items.prompt:
Read the sentence from the passage.
She opened her eyes and imagined all the faces staring at her were cabbages.
What does the phrase faces staring at her were cabbages tell the reader about Samantha?

options.text: Samantha is feeling so nervous that she is starting to see things that aren't really there.
Samantha wants to make herself forget that there are actual people watching her.
Thinking of cabbages has helped Samantha remember her lines before.
There are actors in the play who are pretending to be cabbages.

items.prompt:
The following question has two parts. First, answer part A. Then, answer part B.

Part A

Choose the sentence that best explains what the use of dialogue tells about the relationship between Samantha and her family and friends.

options.text: Samantha's friends and family try to help her not to worry.
Samantha's family will feel upset if she chooses not to perform.
Samantha's friends and family want to help her get out of the play.
Samantha's friends want her to feel better but are worried themselves.

items.prompt:
Part B

Which lines from the passage support your answer in part A? Select all that apply.

options.text: "Why not?" asked Samantha's older sister, Megan. "You said it was the best part in the whole play."
"My stomach feels funny," said Samantha.
"You'll be wonderful," said Mom.
"Patrick, are you afraid to go onstage?" asked Samantha.
"My big brother told me to pretend that the people in the audience are cabbages," answered Anika.
"I'm a healthy tooth," she began.

items.prompt: What does the author do by having Samantha ask the same question three times to friends? Pick two choices.

options.text: The author proves that Samantha has a lot of friends.
The author makes the reader wonder if Samantha feels nervous.
The author shows that Samantha does not believe what her friends tell her.
The author shows how much Samantha wants to stop feeling afraid to go on stage.
The author shows the reader that Samantha needs too much help from other people.
The author shows that there can be different ways to stop feeling scared of something.

items.prompt:
The following question has two parts. First, answer part A. Then, answer part B.

Part A

Which sentence best describes the lesson Samantha learns in the story?

options.text: It is exciting to be the star of the show.
It is okay to quit at something if we cannot do it.
Doing what others say to do will always help us.
The people who care about us can help us through hard times.

items.prompt:
Part B

Which sentence(s) from the passage best supports your answer in part A?

options.text: "I don't want to be the healthy tooth anymore," said Samantha as she walked up the sidewalk to the Multipurpose Room.
Samantha walked slowly to the center of the stage and looked out at all the people. Everyone was quiet.
She opened her eyes and imagined all the faces staring at her were cabbages. She still couldn't remember her lines.
She found them sitting in the second row. She smiled, and they all smiled back.

items.prompt:
Read the paragraphs from the passage.
Samantha walked slowly to the center of the stage and looked out at all the people. Everyone was quiet. The lights made her face feel tight and hot.

Samantha couldn't remember what to say. She closed her eyes and pretended she was at practice and nobody was watching. Her legs wobbled.

She opened her eyes and imagined all the faces staring at her were cabbages. She still couldn't remember her lines. Her hands sweated.
What can the reader conclude about how Samantha is feeling in these paragraphs? Include information from the passage in your answer.
Why is using a chronological order of events important in understanding the climax in the passage?

options.text: It helps the reader to understand the steps Samantha makes toward finally giving her performance.
It helps the reader to learn how the teacher runs the play for Samantha and the other students.
It helps the reader to understand how Samantha studies her lines for the play.
It helps the reader to get to know each of Samantha's friends.

items.prompt:
Read the sentences from the passage.
Samantha's teacher waited just inside. "Here's our healthy tooth!" she said, leading Samantha backstage.

"Break a leg," said Megan.

"You'll be wonderful," said Mom.

"We'll be watching," said Dad.

"Watch out for Tooth Decay," said Brian.
What does the author tell the reader by using break a leg?

options.text: Megan hopes Samantha will trip onstage.
Megan thinks Samantha will forget her lines.
Megan supports her sister with an expression for good luck.
Megan tries to give her sister and excuse to skip her performance.

items.prompt: Which sentence from the passage best shows how Samantha overcomes her fear of performing?

options.text: "Mrs. Johnson picked you because she knows you can do it," said Dad as he opened the door.
Samantha walked slowly to the center of the stage and looked out at all the people.
She needed to find her family in the audience and pretend she was having fun practicing with them.
"I am a healthy tooth," she began.

items.prompt: Samantha is nervous about her performance. Pick four details from the passage that best support this idea.

options.text: Her face feels hot.
She practices at night.
Her stomach feels funny.
She forgets what to say at first.
She asks her friends if they are afraid.
Her costume is flapping against her legs.

items.prompt: What happens after Samantha walks to the center of the stage? Use key events from the passage in your answer.
